title: US Fighter Pilots Witness UFO (ft. Ryan Graves)
summary: Former Navy F-18 pilot Ryan Graves discusses his firsthand experiences detecting and observing UAPs off the Virginia coast during 2014-2015, describing objects with anomalous flight characteristics including stationary hovering in high winds, supersonic speeds without visible propulsion, and geometric shapes (cubes inside spheres). He also reflects on his historic Congressional testimony alongside David Fravor and David Grusch, and discusses the broader implications for pilot safety and government transparency.

acronyms.definition: Term used to describe unidentified objects observed in the air, replacing the older term UFO in official contexts
Mechanically scanned array radar system used in F-18 aircraft with a moving dish that sends and receives radar signals
Actively electronically scanned array (AESA) radar system, a fixed and more precise radar upgrade for F-18 aircraft
Advanced radar technology that is fixed and can send energy in smaller, more precise packets
Infrared and electro-optical targeting system used to detect heat signatures
Highly classified government programs with restricted access
Safety paperwork filed when a hazardous incident occurs during flight operations
US intelligence agency responsible for satellite reconnaissance
US agency focused on oceanic and atmospheric science and monitoring
Term for unidentified objects observed underwater
Official responsible for oversight of the US intelligence community
Secure room for discussing classified information

events.description: Nimitz carrier strike group UAP encounter involving Commander David Fravor
Ryan Graves' squadron began detecting UAPs with upgraded APG-79 radar systems off Virginia coast
New York Times article on UAPs was published, prompting wider discussion among pilots
Congressional hearing where Ryan Graves, David Fravor, and David Grusch testified about UAPs
Drones swarmed Langley Air Force Base for 17 days, forcing relocation of F-22s
Robert Sarbacher allegedly helped set up UFO secrecy with Robert J. Oppenheimer at the Atomic Energy Commission

key_claims.claim: UAP sightings are routine, not rare or isolated, in US military airspace
Objects detected were stationary in Category 3 hurricane-type winds without any visible propulsion
Objects were detected traveling at speeds up to Mach 1.2 without exhaust plumes
A pilot described seeing a dark gray or black cube inside a clear sphere, approximately 5-15 feet in diameter
Three different sensor systems (radar, FLIR, and AIM-9X missiles) correlated to confirm physical objects were present
There were at least five unreported near mid-air collisions with UAPs in Ryan Graves' squadron
About half the pilots in a ready room raised their hands acknowledging they had seen cubes and spheres when the topic was raised
UAP sightings have occurred at virtually every major US Navy base including Norfolk, California, Fallon, and Pax River
David Grusch provided over 1,000 pages of documentation to the IC Inspector General who deemed it 'urgent and credible'
David Grusch brought 40 people who claim to have worked on crash retrieval programs firsthand to the IC Inspector General
Hyman Rickover told Thomas Townsend Brown that the Navy had already figured out gravity manipulation technology
F-22s had to be moved from Langley Air Force Base due to drone incursions
